cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what we learn about Puya chilensis in England from the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go through the passage again carefully. The plant is in a greenhouse in Surrey, planted 15 years ago. The Royal Horticultural Society feeds it liquid fertilizer. The passage mentions that in its natural habitat, it uses spikes to trap animals, but in England, they use liquid fertilizer instead. The RHS is giving it liquid fertilizer because feeding it on its natural diet (trapping sheep) might be problematic. Looking at the options: A) feeds on man-made liquid fertilizer. The passage says they feed it liquid fertilizer, so this seems correct. The RHS mentions using liquid fertilizer to avoid the natural method of trapping animals. B) often kills sheep and other animals. But the passage says that in England, the plant's spikes are out of reach of both children and sheep. Also, the natural trapping is something that happens in its native habitat, not necessarily in England. So B is probably incorrect here. C) has once bloomed 15 years before. The plant was planted 15 years ago, but the bloom is happening now for the first time after over a decade. So 15 years ago would be when it was planted, not when it bloomed. The first bloom is now, so C is incorrect. D) uses
